title: Omission of OpenTofu fork and modern state management paradigms

evidence.heading: # Terraform
evidence.excerpt: Terraform is an open-source infrastructure as code (IaC) tool developed by HashiCorp

recommendation: Acknowledge the OpenTofu fork in the introduction due to the 2023 license change. Update state management guidance to emphasize remote backends, state locking, and directory-based workspace patterns over legacy workspaces. Introduce Terragrunt for DRY configuration management.

title: Lack of IaC testing and policy-as-code tooling references

evidence.heading: # Terraform
evidence.excerpt: Terraform's state management and modular structure allow for efficient scaling

recommendation: Add references to industry-standard IaC testing and validation tools such as Terratest, tflint, Checkov, or OPA/Sentinel. Modern production workflows require automated linting, security scanning, and integration tests before deployment.

title: Outdated Microsoft Documentation Link Domain
recommendation: Update the link to use the learn.microsoft.com domain, which is Microsoft's current documentation standard. Consider supplementing with modern cloud-native design principles like Infrastructure as Code (IaC), GitOps, and Platform Engineering patterns.

title: Typo in repository owner name in link text
recommendation: Correct the typo in the link display text from 'bitname' to 'bitnami' to match the actual GitHub organization name and maintain consistency.

title: Lack of modern IaC and GitOps paradigms

evidence.heading: # Configuration Management
evidence.excerpt: This post focuses on its modern application and use in agile CI/CD software environments.

recommendation: Expand the content to explicitly cover declarative configuration management, Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools (Terraform, Pulumi), GitOps workflows, and cloud-native configuration patterns (e.g., Kustomize, Helm, Crossplane) to align with current industry standards.
